author: Dr. David Park, PGY-1 / Dr. Maria Castro, PGY-3 / Dr. Sarah Beckman (Attending)
content:
ADMISSION HISTORY AND PHYSICAL — JOHNS HOPKINS HOSPITAL
Date/Time: 3/15/2027, 4:30 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
DOB: 8/12/2003 (Age 23)
Admission source: Emergency Department (presented at 12:45 PM)
Admitting team: General Medicine (Dr. Beckman attending, Dr. Castro PGY-3, Dr. Park PGY-1)
Interpreter: Spanish (Language Line); mother and aunt at bedside.

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
'No me puedo mover, no me puedo comer' — 'I can't move, I can't eat.'

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:

Ms. Sandoval is a 23-year-old Spanish-speaking woman with known active SLE (with new inflammatory myositis per 2/8/2027 admission), Hashimoto's thyroiditis, and a long history of fragmented care, who presents from her home directly to the ED via her mother and aunt (brought in by family vehicle, not EMS).

Course since AMA discharge 2/11/2027 (per available records and family report):

- 2/15/2027: Hopkins rheumatology follow-up (Dr. Tanaka). Continued prednisone 30 mg, HCQ 200 BID, MMF 1 g BID, levothyroxine. Weight 41.6 kg. CK 1490, ESR 58, anti-dsDNA 36, albumin 2.4. Skin biopsy from 2/10 preliminary read returned 2/12.

- 2/22/2027: SCHEDULED EMG and muscle biopsy NOT performed. Patient too weak to leave the apartment (could not get down the 3 flights of stairs). Mother called to reschedule; appointment moved to 3/8/2027.

- Late February through early March: Progressive worsening at home per mother:
  * Dysphagia: now profound. Patient can no longer manage pureed solids without choking. Has been on liquids and small amounts of Ensure for ~10 days. Last solid food was ~3/3/2027 (a small piece of soft bread that she choked on; family afraid to feed her solids since). Mother estimates intake of ~400-600 kcal/day, mostly Ensure and small sips of broth.
  * Weakness: profound. Patient is now bedbound. Unable to rise from bed without 2-person assistance. Cannot lift arms to brush her hair. Speaks softly with nasal quality. Voice now described as 'whispery.'
  * Weight: family has not had access to scale; patient looks emaciated to mother.
  * Skin: scaling has WORSENED markedly. Now diffuse pinkish-brown scaling over elbows, knees, shins, dorsum of hands, AND has spread to the trunk in patches. Mother describes peeling, flaking skin in some areas. Hair has continued to thin and now appears very brittle, with patches of lighter color at the base of strands.
  * Edema: significant. Mother says patient's face is 'puffy' all day now, not just in the mornings; ankles swollen; abdomen looks 'bloated.'
  * Bowel/bladder: bowel movements infrequent (every 3-4 days; small, formed). Urinating less than before but no dysuria.
  * Mental status: family describes patient as 'sad and very tired'; she 'sleeps most of the day.' Not confused. Responds when spoken to.

- 3/8/2027: RESCHEDULED EMG/muscle biopsy NOT performed. Mother attempted to bring patient via Lyft; patient was too weak to transfer to the vehicle. Hopkins rheumatology nursing line spoken to that day; recommended ED evaluation. Family did not bring her in.

- 3/10/2027: Esperanza Health PCP (Dr. Williams) called by mother; he urgently recommended ED evaluation, but mother could not take her at that time.

- 3/12-3/14/2027 (past 3 days): Patient stopped most oral intake. Drinking only small sips of water. Mother reports patient slept >20 hours yesterday. Today she felt patient's arms 'felt cold' and she was 'breathing strange' — somewhat shallow and faster than usual. Mother panicked and brought her in.

Review of systems otherwise denied: fever, cough, chest pain, hemoptysis, hematemesis, melena, hematochezia, abdominal pain, dysuria, gross hematuria, headache, focal weakness on one side, vision changes, seizures.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Systemic lupus erythematosus with inflammatory myositis (per 2/8/2027 admission workup)
- Hashimoto's thyroiditis
- Severe protein-calorie malnutrition (per 2/8/2027 admission)
- Iron-deficiency anemia (2018, resolved)
- Latent TB ruled out (2018)
- Discharged AMA 2/11/2027 from this hospital

MEDICATIONS (verified with family):
- Prednisone 30 mg PO daily (taking; runs out tomorrow, 3/16/2027)
- Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID (taking)
- Mycophenolate mofetil 1 g PO BID (ran out 3/9/2027 — no further supply secured)
- Levothyroxine 25 mcg PO daily (taking inconsistently — "two or three times a week" per mother)
- TMP/SMX 80/400 mg PO M/W/F for PCP prophylaxis (taking when she remembers, last dose ~3/10)
- Thiamine 100 mg PO daily (ran out late Feb)
- Calcium 500 mg BID, Vitamin D 1000 IU daily (taking inconsistently)
- Omeprazole 20 mg PO daily (taking)

ALLERGIES: NKDA.

FAMILY HISTORY: Maternal aunt with SLE; mother with hypothyroidism, pre-diabetes; father (deceased) with treated TB.

SOCIAL HISTORY: Lives with mother, younger brother, maternal aunt in East Baltimore. Not working since November 2026. Insurance: emergency Medicaid coverage active for this admission. Speaks Spanish primarily. Denies tobacco, alcohol, drug use. Has not had sex in over 1 year. No recent travel. Pets: none.

VITALS ON ARRIVAL (12:45 PM, ED):
BP 86/52, HR 124, RR 26, T 97.2 F (36.2 C — mildly hypothermic), SpO2 94% on RA -> 97% on 2L NC. Weight 35.4 kg (down 5.8 kg from 2/11/2027 weight of 41.2 kg; total loss from 1/12/2026 baseline of 50.8 kg = 15.4 kg / 30.3%). Height 150 cm. BMI 15.7. Pain 4/10 (diffuse soreness, weakness > pain).

Updated vitals on arrival to floor (4:15 PM, after 1L NS over 4h):
BP 96/58, HR 110, RR 22, T 97.6 F, SpO2 96% on 2L NC.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

General: SEVERELY CACHECTIC young woman, age-appropriate cognition, mother holding her hand. Visibly emaciated with prominent temporal wasting, sunken cheeks, prominent clavicles. Bilateral lower-extremity and facial edema present despite cachexia. Skin pale; nail beds pale. Alert and oriented x 3 but speaks in soft, nasal, somewhat slow Spanish.

HEAD/EYES: Temporal wasting bilaterally. Conjunctivae very pale. Sclerae anicteric. PERRL. EOM intact but slight bilateral ptosis noted. Mild bilateral periorbital edema.

ORAL/PHARYNX: Lips somewhat cracked and dry but moist on inspection. Several small (~2-3 mm) shallow ulcers on hard palate (3) and tongue (1). Oropharynx without exudate. Voice nasal, somewhat hoarse. No drooling at rest but does not handle her oral secretions perfectly — small amount of pooled saliva noted. Soft palate elevation reduced bilaterally with mild asymmetry on left.

NECK: Trachea midline. No lymphadenopathy. Thyroid not enlarged. JVP not elevated but difficult to assess given edema.

CARDIOVASCULAR: Tachycardic 110-124, regular rhythm. No murmur audible. Distant heart sounds. Capillary refill 4 seconds at the fingertips. Pulses thready but symmetric.

PULMONARY: RR 22-26, shallow respirations. Decreased breath sounds at bilateral bases, no crackles, no wheezing. Cough is weak — patient cannot clear strong cough on command.

ABDOMEN: Distended (anasarca with abdominal wall edema). Soft, non-tender to palpation. Bowel sounds quiet but present. No organomegaly appreciable. Mild pretibial pitting +2 edema; sacral +1 edema.

SKIN:
- Diffuse scaling, flaking, pinkish-brown dermatosis covering the trunk (chest, abdomen, back), extensor surfaces of upper and lower extremities, and dorsum of hands. In some areas there is overlying flaking/desquamation. Skin has a 'crackled' appearance in some areas of the abdomen and thighs.
- Skin overall thin, dry, with reduced turgor.
- Hair: diffuse scalp thinning. On inspection, hair appears brittle and there are bands of color change at the proximal hair shafts.
- Nails: pale, brittle, with mild transverse depressions on several fingernails.
- No discoid lesions. No purpura. No vasculitic ulcers.

MUSCULOSKELETAL:
- Joint exam: no overt synovitis appreciated today (likely masked by edema and steroids); mild tenderness at MCPs only. Joints with reduced range due to edema and weakness more than pain.
- Muscle bulk: visibly reduced; deltoid, quadriceps, gluteal muscle wasting prominent.
- Strength testing (limited by patient effort/fatigue):
  * Hip flexion: 2/5 bilaterally (cannot lift leg off bed against gravity; can slide)
  * Knee extension: 3/5 bilaterally
  * Hip extension: 2/5
  * Shoulder abduction: 2/5 bilaterally
  * Elbow flexion: 3+/5
  * Wrist extension: 4/5
  * Grip strength: 3/5
  * Neck flexion: 2/5 (head lag with attempted sit-up)
  * Distal extremities: 4-5/5 throughout
- Cannot stand from bed without 2-person assist.
- Reflexes 1+ throughout, symmetric.

NEUROLOGIC:
Mental status: Alert, oriented x 3. Engages with interpreter. Affect somewhat flat but appropriate. Speech: dysarthric (nasal, weak). Cranial nerves: CN II - PERRLA; CN III/IV/VI - EOM intact, slight bilateral ptosis; CN V - facial sensation intact; CN VII - face symmetric at rest, but smile and forehead movements weaker bilaterally; CN VIII - hearing grossly intact; CN IX/X - palate elevation reduced and slightly asymmetric, gag reflex sluggish; CN XI - shoulder shrug 3+/5; CN XII - tongue protrudes midline with reduced excursion. Sensation: intact to light touch, pinprick, vibration symmetrically. Coordination: cannot perform finger-to-nose smoothly due to weakness. Gait: cannot ambulate.

ED COURSE TODAY (summary):
12:45 PM: ESI 2 triage. Moved to critical care bay.
13:00: IV access x 2. Initial labs drawn (see LAB012). Cardiac monitor.
13:15: Initial labs return.
13:30: Cautious 500 mL bolus NS started (rate-limited given edema). NPO confirmed.
14:00-15:00: CXR (clear, low-volume), CT chest/abd/pelvis (see IMG004), EKG (sinus tachy 118, no acute changes).
15:30: NPO maintained. Thiamine 200 mg IV given BEFORE any dextrose. Multivitamin IV. Cautious electrolyte repletion (K 10 mEq IV; Mg 2 g IV).
16:00: Transferred to medicine floor with telemetry.

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
23F admitted from home in extremis: severe weight loss (BMI 15.7, 30.3% loss from baseline), anasarca, pancytopenia (Hgb 7.8, WBC 2.3, Plt 122), severe hypoalbuminemia (1.6), profound proximal weakness, oropharyngeal dysphagia, scaling dermatosis, mild hypothermia, oral ulcers. Known active SLE; medication adherence has been intermittent. Multiple electrolyte derangements at admission (Na 130, K 3.0, Mg 1.4, Ca 7.8, Phos 1.8).

ADMISSION ORDERS:
- Admit to medicine; telemetry; close monitoring (consider ICU upgrade if unstable).
- NPO. Aspiration precautions; HOB up 30 degrees; suction at bedside.
- Strict I/O, daily weights, ortho vitals.
- DVT prophylaxis with mechanical compression only (no chemoprophylaxis pending platelet trend).
- IVF: 0.9% NS at 50 mL/hr (cautious given edema). NO dextrose-containing fluids until thiamine repleted.
- Thiamine 200 mg IV daily x 5 days, multivitamin IV daily, folic acid 1 mg IV daily.
- Q6h K/Mg/Phos labs x first 72h; repeat CMP, CBC daily.
- Hold MMF. Continue HCQ 200 BID and prednisone 30 mg daily (via NG once placed).
- Hold further immunosuppression escalation until infection screen results return.
- Consults placed: rheumatology, neurology (EMG/biopsy planning), GI/nutrition, infectious disease, dermatology, speech-language pathology (MBSS).
- Pre-immunosuppression infection screen sent: TB-IGRA, HBV/HCV/HIV, CMV PCR, EBV PCR, beta-glucan, galactomannan, strongyloides Ab. Blood cultures x 2 sent.
- Echo and 24-hour urine ordered.
- Code status: FULL CODE (confirmed with patient + family).
- Anticipated LOS: 10-14 days minimum.

Documented by Dr. Park (PGY-1); reviewed and modified by Dr. Castro (PGY-3); co-signed by Dr. Beckman (attending). Total documentation time including bedside interview with interpreter: 95 minutes.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. David Park, MD (PGY-1, Internal Medicine) — primary author
Dr. Maria Castro, MD (PGY-3, Internal Medicine) — co-author and edits
Dr. Sarah Beckman, MD (Attending, Internal Medicine) — reviewing attending
Date: 3/15/2027
